title: Introduction To Internet Of Things - Week 5
subject: Introduction To Internet Of Things

questions.question: What does the term "Interoperability" refer to in the context of IoT?
options: a. The ability of a system to operate without power
b. The ability of different systems/products to work together without restrictions
c. The ability to increase the speed of the internet

questions.question: In the collaborative concept (Cosine) theory for interoperability, an object is defined as a quadruple (A, B, C, D). What does 'C' represent?
options: d. The definition of the object

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the "Syntactic Interoperability" challenge?
options: a. Devices having different physical shapes
b. Devices using different message formats and data structures
c. Devices located in different countries

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary reason for the popularity of Arduino in IoT implementations?
options: a) It is highly expensive and exclusive
b) It is low resource consuming and cheap
c) It requires high-power external cooling
d) It only supports digital signals

questions.question: What is the clock speed of the Arduino Uno board?
Which function is used to iteratively loop over instances in an Arduino sketch?
In Arduino, what is the default starting index for an array?
What does the delay(5000) command do in a traffic signal simulation?
options: b) Introduces a delay of 5 seconds
c) Turns off all LEDs immediately
d) Skips the current execution loop

questions.question: Which header file must be called to use mathematical functions like sin() or cos()?
The DHT22 sensor is used for measuring which physical quantities?
What is the recommended voltage range for the VCC pin of a DHT sensor to avoid damage?
When integrating a typical digital temperature and humidity sensor (like the DHT series) with an Arduino board, which Arduino pin connection is used to receive the sensor's data?
Which of the following describes the primary function of an actuator?
options: b. Converts energy or signals into motion

questions.question: When using the Servo library in Arduino, which function is used to specify the pin where the servo is connected?
In the context of the Arduino Servo library, which of the following is the correct function to command the servo motor to rotate to a specific angle?
